sections: HCM-0.7 implementation-program and first-packet proof gate

repo_truth: Review the exact seven-entry uncommitted documentation/planning subject over baseline HEAD 367a969c578e34118bb6f4b2f4655811229bb223. Current Rust still uses the fixed CanonicalArtifactKind and product paths; HCM-0.7 may plan but must not execute HCM-1.1 or promote runtime gates. Derive the verdict from live authority and replayed bytes.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay every sorted subject-manifest entry plus aggregate before review.
Review the HCM-0.7 canonical status/map/ledger changes, complete HCM-1.1 SPEC/plan/todo packet, and proof wall.
Read unchanged canonical 01-05, HCM-0.6 decision, live engine/Cargo sources, and official dependency documentation only as needed to validate authority and feasibility.
Run read-only Markdown, link, Git, schema/handoff, source, dependency-feature, security, and semantic assertion checks needed to evaluate the subject.
Return findings first through the built-in channel with exact file and line evidence.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user-task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, runtime asset, generated schema, first-party kind/profile/instance, intake, renderer, Projection, CLI, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, contract, dock, or later-slice implementation.
Do not start HCM-1.1 or treat packet approval as implementation completion.
Do not infer missing HCM-0.6 subordinate schemas, capability contracts, intake/renderer definitions, profile publication, or content fields.
Do not require a conventional alternative without identifying a violated authority, invariant, proof gate, scope boundary, or concrete feasibility defect.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact sorted seven-entry manifest and aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Each actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated contract/invariant/gate, why the boundary or proof is wrong, smallest valid remediation, and missing verification.

Verify completed HCM-0.2/.3/.4/.5/.6/.8 evidence, abandoned HCM-0.9 treatment, Phase 0 closure, strict phase/list sequencing, packet authorization, and no accidental next-slice execution are coherent with canonical authority.

Verify HCM-1.1 is the correct first implementation slice and that SPEC, plan, todo, 00, 04, and 06 agree on objective, owner, baseline, exact scope, dependencies, source/asset topology, task ordering, proof, classification ceiling, non-goals, and stop conditions.

Verify the packet is additive and preserves CanonicalArtifactKind, canonical layout, setup, doctor, flow, HCM-1.2 through HCM-1.4 ownership, HCM-0.6 unpublished subordinate definitions, and current runtime/open-gate truth.

Verify exact identity, RFC 8785/SHA-256 typed closure, duplicate-key refusal, schema closure/ref normalization, path/no-follow/bounds rules, Draft 2020-12 validation, in-memory-only resolution, dependency feature posture, typed errors, and no remote/ambient/executable behavior are implementable and contract-correct.

Verify stable-role assets and fingerprints, capability/kind meta-validation, source ordering, public API boundary, package-content proof, and the capability-free custom fixture neither invent later definitions nor smuggle product adoption.
Verify HCM-1.1 may close PG-KIND-01 only; PG-KIND-02 stays open until supplied intake coverage is exercised by its owning later slice, with no contradictory closure wording remaining.
Verify TDD increments and positive/negative/security/package/workspace proof are sufficient to make future HCM-1.1 independently implementable and reviewable without widening authority.
Replay the proof wall sufficiently to identify fabricated, incomplete, stale, circular, or untracked-byte assertions and verify exact docs-only scope.
Return CLEAN only if there is no valid Critical or Required finding; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest, aggregate, required authority, or a required read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than resolving any missing product/subordinate decision by inference.
Return to the parent if correction would require Rust/runtime implementation, first-party definition publication, a later slice, or authority outside HCM-0.7 documentation/planning scope.
